title: France will likely face third heatwave of the year this weekend and next week
subhead: Temperatures are set to drop tomorrow and Thursday across the country, before rising again Friday in the southern half. The duration, intensity and geographical extent of the episode remain uncertain at this stage.

Spain has three months to review one million applications for migrant regularization
subhead: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government is defending a measure aimed at supporting economic growth amid a 'demographic winter.' This is Spain's seventh such initiative in 40 years. Initially, the plan was met with broad consensus, but it has since become a source of division.

title: Multiple raids conducted over alleged far-right embezzlement at European Parliament
subhead: The probe is based on a report that claimed that far-right members of the Parliament 'unduly spent' €4.33 million. The raids notably targeted a key contractor for the French far-right Rassemblement National party.

title: Mbappé fires France into World Cup last 16
subhead: France's victory over Sweden on Tuesday has elevated prolific striker Kylian Mbappé to an incredible World Cup record:  18 in 18 matches – only one behind Argentina's Lionel Messi's all-time record of 19 goals.

title: 250 years of American independence: Sinclair Lewis, the Nobel Prize-winning author who 'burst the smugness' of the average American
subhead: 'America 250' (9/13). The 'Roaring Twenties' saw consumerism, puritanism, and xenophobia rise together in the United States. Sinclair Lewis, a native of Sauk Centre, a rural town in Minnesota, rose to fame with his biting satires of American life, which still resonate today.

subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.

title: The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
subhead: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.

title: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
subhead: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.
